spec_match.rationale: The timing chart is now driven by a Deno streamed HTML route that sends delayed <template for> row patches.

interactive.rationale: The delay control changes the streamed response and the byte log, but native iframe patching still needs chrome-devtools-mcp verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now frames timing in terms of parser patches, not ReadableStream reader dispatch.

edge_cases.rationale: The route deliberately delays chunk 2 while later chunk templates flush first.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus and the WICG patching explainer.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables and the common live stream control surface.

openQuestions.title: Verify native parser patching in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to confirm timing rows patch in the iframe before the delayed chunk 2 route completes.

summary: Rebuilt the timing concept around actual delayed template patches from the server.
